2606.22272.title: MixedPEFT: Combining Multiple PEFT Methods with Mixed Objectives for Unsupervised Domain Adaptation
2606.22272.paper_summary: Pre-trained language models struggle when applied to new domains, as full fine-tuning is computationally expensive and prone to catastrophic forgetting. This study addresses this challenge by presenting a novel parameter-efficient strategy for unsupervised domain adaptation that combines custom PEFT architectures with mixed-objective training. Our approach simultaneously optimizes classification performance on labeled source domain data and masked language modeling (MLM) on unlabeled target domain data, preserving target domain knowledge while adapting to source domain tasks. Our method employs a custom union of invertible adapters and Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) within a unified parameter-efficient framework. Through comprehensive evaluation on the Multi-Genre Natural Language Inference (MNLI) dataset across 20 domain shifts, our approach achieves significant improvements over existing methods: 1.41 percentage points over the current parameter-efficient state-of-the-art UDapter, 1.26 percentage points over the fully-tuned DANN baseline, and 0.86 percentage points over DSN, while utilizing only 7% of the model's trainable parameters. These results establish new benchmarks for parameter-efficient unsupervised domain adaptation and demonstrate that carefully designed PEFT combinations with concurrent optimization can outperform both existing parameter-efficient methods and traditional fully-tuned approaches.
2606.22272.paper_summary_zh: 預訓練語言模型在應用於新領域時面臨挑戰，因為完全微調計算成本高且容易造成災難性遺忘。本研究通過提出一種新穎的參數高效策略來解決這一挑戰，該策略結合了自定義的PEFT架構和混合目標訓練。我們的方法同時優化標記源域數據上的分類性能和未標記目標域數據上的掩碼語言建模（MLM），在適應源域任務的同時保留目標域知識。我們的方法在統一的參數高效框架內使用了可逆適配器和低秩適應（LoRA）的自定義聯合。通過在20個領域轉移的多類型自然語言推理（MNLI）數據集上進行全面評估，我們的方法在現有方法上實現了顯著改進：比當前參數高效的最先進UDapter高出1.41個百分點，比完全微調的DANN基線高出1.26個百分點，比DSN高出0.86個百分點，同時僅使用模型可訓練參數的7%。這些結果為參數高效的無監督領域適應建立了新的基準，並證明精心設計的PEFT組合與並行優化可以超越現有的參數高效方法和傳統的完全微調方法。
2606.22272.author: Mohammed Rawhani et.al.
2606.22272.authors: Mohammed Rawhani, Dervis Karaboga, Ozkan Ufuk Nalbantoglu, Alper Basturk, Bahriye Akay
